question:
Will Netherlands win on 2025-11-17?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 17, 2025
If Netherlands wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: fifa.com

evidences.title: Netherlands vs Morocco FIFA World Cup 2026 Preview ... - Goal.com
evidences.content: The Netherlands’ tactical blueprint under Koeman relies heavily on fluid, attacking transitions and isolating wingers in one-on-one scenarios. With dynamic midfield progressors like Tijjani Reijnders and Ryan Gravenberch looking to break the lines, the Oranje try to overload the central channels before unlocking wide outlets like Cody Gakpo. Morocco, conversely, values a deeply disciplined mid-block that prioritizes defensive compactness over aggressive pressing. The Atlas Lions absorb sustained periods of pressure, counting on the immense work rate of Sofyan Amrabat to clog passing lanes before turning possession over to creative spark Brahim Díaz to initiate rapid forward surges. ## Defensive configurations face ultimate pressure [...] Morocco, meanwhile, showed immense tactical resilience to secure their place in the Round of 32 out of a grueling Group C.The Atlas Lions made a major statement in their opening fixture by holding eventual group-winners Brazil to a stubborn 1-1 draw in East Rutherford. Building on that defensive foundation, they executed their game plans clinically across their remaining group matches to book their date in Monterrey. While the Dutch team's fluid...

evidences.title: How Ronald Koeman's Tactics Solved Japan… Almost | Netherlands vs Japan World Cup Tactical Analysis
evidences.content: knockdowns and shots. Still, the key factor was Japan's defensive structure. Japan defended in a 5-3-2 [music] shape, but their center-backs were always prepared to step out aggressively and engage opponents higher up the pitch. Their backline played proactively, and the entire team remained extremely compact. Reijnders and Gravenberch simply had no chance of receiving in central areas between the lines. The Netherlands circulated possession, but Japan's organization was exceptional. One sequence perfectly demonstrated their discipline. Cody Gakpo made a run in behind, and the right center-back, who had initially been monitoring Gravenberch in central areas, was immediately ready to drop deeper and cover the space. Japan switched references brilliantly, constantly protecting dangerous [...] wing-back, Nakamura. In the mid-block, Japan's shape often resembled more of a 4-4-2 than a 5-3-2 because Nakamura was willing to step higher and closely track Summerville, preventing him from receiving freely. For example, when Van Dijk gave the ball away, Japan immediately recovered possession [music] and launched a four-man counter-attack. That pattern was characteristic throughout the mat...

The Barcelona midfielder’s technical ability, vision, and understanding of the game make him essential to Oranje’s attacking play. De Jong’s capacity to control matches and create opportunities for teammates has been instrumental in the Netherlands’ recent success. His performances at Euro 2024 demonstrated his readiness for the World Cup stage. evidences.title: FIFA World Cup 2026™ | Fixtures, groups, teams & more
evidences.content: Explore details Image 13: FIFA ## FIFA World Cup 2026 Official Match BallFIFA World Cup 2026 Official Match Ball Image 14 The adidas TRIONDA was unveiled as the Official Match Ball for FIFA World Cup 26 on 2 October 2025. The name can be translated from Spanish as “three waves”, and the ball’s vibrant design features a red, green and blue colour scheme that pays homage to the three host countries, while a brand-new four-panel construction with fluid geometry replicates the waves referenced in the ball’s name. Iconography that represents each host country adorns the ball, with the maple leaf for Canada, an eagle for Mexico and a star for the United States, while gold embellishments pay tribute to the FIFA World Cup Trophy. [...] ## FIFA World Cup 2026 mascotsFIFA World Cup 2026 mascots Image 15 The three official mascots, each highlighting one of the host nations, were unveiled in September 2025. Maple™ the Moose (Canada), Zayu™ the Jaguar (Mexico) and Clutch™ the Bald Eagle (United States) have been thoughtfully developed to reflect the vibrant culture, heritage and spirit of their respective countries, coming together to symbolise unity, diversity and the shared passion for the...

evidences.title: Netherlands v Morocco | World Cup Round of 32 | Match preview
evidences.content: Close Related Content Close Share The breathtaking Monterrey Stadiumwill host its fourth and final match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 when Netherlands face Morocco. It was not a happy hunting ground for another North African side, with Tunisia losing 5-1 to Sweden and 4-0 to Japan in Group F. The third game was South Africa's stunning 1-0 Group A victory over Korea Republic, which won Bafana Bafana a spot in the Round of 32. Mexican side Monterrey play their football in this modern-day marvel, which is equipped with compact energy solutions and low voltage equipment. The venue also hosted eight games at the 2022 Concacaf W Championship, including USA's 1-0 final victory over Canada. Coldplay, Bad Bunny and Justin Bieber have performed at the ground in its opening decade. evidences.title: 2024-2025 Laws of the Game changes in football - Dutch Referee Blog
evidences.content: making you a better referee # 2024-2025 Laws of the Game changes in football 2024-2025 Laws of the Game changes in football are announced. Below you’ll find an outline summary of Law changes. No big changes. Most interesting for referees is info non-deliberate handball and DOGSO, shin guards (but remains quite general) and different approach on encroachment in line with VAR.
